# 4. Structural Diagrams & Schematics

## 4.1 Predictor-Corrector Method — Operational Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Start: Given y_n, y_n-1, y_n-2, y_n-3] --> B[Compute slopes f_n-3, f_n-2, f_n-1, f_n]
    B --> C[Apply Adams-Bashforth Predictor Formula]
    C --> D[Obtain y_n+1 predicted value]
    D --> E[Compute predicted slope f_n+1 p]
    E --> F[Apply Adams-Moulton Corrector Formula]
    F --> G[Obtain y_n+1 corrected value]
    G --> H{Convergence Check}
    H -->|Not Converged| F
    H -->|Converged| I[Output y_n+1]
    I --> J[Increment n: Move to next step]
    J --> B
```

## 4.2 Linear Shooting Method — Block Architecture Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[BVP Input y prime prime equals f, Boundary at a and b] --> B[Formulate IVP 1 with u prime a equals 0]
    A --> C[Formulate IVP 2 with v prime a equals 1]
    B --> D[Solve IVP 1 using RK4 to get u of b]
    C --> E[Solve IVP 2 using RK4 to get v of b]
    D --> F[Compute initial slope m equals beta minus u of b divided by v of b]
    E --> F
    F --> G[Solve combined IVP with y prime a equals m]
    G --> H[Output Final Solution y of x]
    H --> I[Verify Boundary Condition at x equals b]
```

## 4.3 Finite Difference Method — Discretization & Solution Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Continuous ODE y double prime equals f of x, y, y prime] --> B[Define grid with step h]
    B --> C[Apply Central Difference Formulas for y prime and y double prime]
    C --> D[Generate Tridiagonal System of Equations]
    D --> E[Apply Boundary Conditions y_0 and y_n]
    E --> F[Solve using Thomas Algorithm]
    F --> G[Forward Sweep: Eliminate sub-diagonal]
    G --> H[Back Substitution: Compute y_n-1 to y_1]
    H --> I[Output Approximate y values at all grid points]
```

## 4.4 Comparison: When to Use Each Method

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[Differential Equation Problem] --> B{Type of Problem?}
    B -->|Initial Value Problem IVP| C{Single-step or Multi-step?}
    C -->|Multi-step Higher Accuracy| D[Predictor-Corrector Method]
    C -->|Simple Self-starting| E[Euler or RK4]
    B -->|Boundary Value Problem BVP| F{Preferred Approach?}
    F -->|Convert to IVP| G[Shooting Method]
    F -->|Direct Discretization| H[Finite Difference Method]
    D --> I[Trajectory, Control Systems]
    G --> J[Beam Deflection, Heat Transfer]
    H --> K[Structural Analysis, PDEs]
```

## 4.5 Milne's Predictor-Corrector — Step-wise Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Input y_n, y_n-1, y_n-2, y_n-3 and corresponding f values] --> B[Milne Predictor: y_n+1 p equals y_n-3 + 4h by 3 times 2f_n minus f_n-1 + 2f_n-2]
    B --> C[Compute f_n+1 p equals f of x_n+1, y_n+1 p]
    C --> D[Milne Corrector: y_n+1 equals y_n-1 + h by 3 times f_n+1 p + 4f_n + f_n-1]
    D --> E{Stability Check}
    E -->|Unstable Oscillations| F[Use Adams-Bashforth-Moulton Instead]
    E -->|Stable| G[Accept y_n+1]
```
